qa_items.likely_question: Is the clogging index pressure-calibrated, and can it be interpreted as a safety limit?
qa_items.short_answer: No. The structural index is not pressure-calibrated; Supplementary Table S19 separates pressure-free structural screening, closure proxies, scalar conductance tests and returned cropped-domain OpenFOAM checks so that the current evidence is not overread as validation-grade flow evidence.
evidence: latex/main.tex: Methods and Results hydraulic-consequence checks
qa_items.safe_wording: structural pre-screening index with a bounded pressure/flow evidence ladder

qa_items.likely_question: Do the current simulations demonstrate a critical clogging transition?
qa_items.short_answer: No. The current cases support a pre-clogging migration/filtering regime.

qa_items.likely_question: Are the voxel fields experimental CT data?
qa_items.short_answer: No. They are DEM-derived voxel reconstructions from simulated particle coordinates.

qa_items.likely_question: Does the model resolve local purge-gas redistribution inside the pore space?
qa_items.short_answer: No. The current model uses one-way Stokes drag; Supplementary Table S18 reports the dimensional debris diameter, nominal particle-Reynolds-number and drag-to-weight range so that the force scale is auditable, while coupled pore-flow remains future work.
evidence: latex/main.tex: DEM debris transport and voxel analysis workflow
qa_items.safe_wording: one-way Stokes drag with a dimensional drag-scaling audit

qa_items.likely_question: Do the localized internal-release cases establish a source-position or source-strength law?
qa_items.short_answer: No. Three localized-release production rows reached their target windows and support retained-bulk/sparse-front decoupling, but they do not define a source-position or source-strength law.
qa_items.safe_wording: target-time mechanism evidence for retained-bulk/sparse-front decoupling

qa_items.likely_question: Can the three loading states be interpreted as a monotonic law?
qa_items.short_answer: No. They are single-seed bounded loading responses with different injection histories.
evidence: latex/main.tex: Debris loading amplifies local accumulation

qa_items.likely_question: Are near-threshold repeat-seed results available, and are partial repeat runs cited as evidence?
qa_items.short_answer: Yes. Three formal near-threshold repeat-seed rows have been post-processed and are used only to bound stochastic variability, not to define a sharp transition.

qa_items.likely_question: How is this work positioned relative to packed-bed deposition, clogging and morphology studies?
qa_items.short_answer: It integrates BTC, retention, local blockage and DEM-derived topology in a bounded pre-clogging framework.
qa_items.avoid_wording: numerical validation against experiments or CFD

qa_items.likely_question: Does the available parameter evidence define a phase map, full factorial sweep or universal critical-clogging diagram?
qa_items.short_answer: No. Supplementary Fig. S25 is a discrete parameter-evidence coverage map that records which drive-state, loading-state and localized-source points support the bounded mechanism interpretation.
evidence: latex/main.tex: mechanism synthesis paragraph citing Supplementary Fig. S25
qa_items.avoid_wording: phase map or full factorial critical-clogging diagram
